refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use, ensuring data acquisition, processing, and dissemination meet the latency target.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the strict latency requirement (p95 ≤ 1.0 s), the overall update process is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, processing, and dissemination, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data capture to model update availability under representative operational scenarios; verify p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency budget.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model with acquired data within latency budget.
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency budget.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-throughput, low-latency telemetry data streams with prioritized network protocols.
DP2: Implement incremental model update algorithms optimized for real-time processing.
DP3: Employ event-driven dissemination mechanisms with multicast or publish-subscribe patterns.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address acquisition, processing, and dissemination respectively.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry acquisition and processing modules due to latency constraints.
Dependency of dissemination timing on processing completion.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected but manageable with clear interface definitions and asynchronous event handling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports required throughput and latency.
Risk of processing delays due to unexpected data volume spikes.
Risk of dissemination bottlenecks if subscriber modules are slow.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor latency performance during integration; consider fallback or buffering strategies if latency targets are missed.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry acquisition, model processing, and dissemination.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data communication between NGHE Solution and external modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data flow, minimizing diffusion.
Primarily data source; limited containment of update logic.
Consumes updated model; no update logic containment.
May receive safety alerts but not involved in update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency-critical functions within NGHE Solution module optimizes latency and reduces diffusion risks.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded.
Risk of interface mismatches causing latency spikes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure NGHE Solution module is architected for scalability and robust interface handling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into measurable sub-functions; selected DPs logically address each sub-FR with feasible solutions.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution module.
Interfaces are well-defined and align with known interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as appropriate ownership boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end within NGHE Solution module using telemetry and tasking interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-critical functions to NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor latency performance during integration testing.
Validate interface timing and throughput under load.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy latency requirement with clear ownership and manageable risks; ready to proceed to detailed design and verification.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency budget.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model with acquired data within latency budget.
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency budget.

revise.why: No immediate revisions needed; continue with detailed design and verification.

assumptions: Network infrastructure meets throughput and latency requirements.
Telemetry data volume remains within expected bounds.
open_risks: Processing delays due to data spikes.
Interface timing mismatches causing latency violations.
evidence_needed: Network performance metrics under operational load.
